overlap_notes: Whole-blood IBD methylation EWAS. Count as an independent epigenomics cohort; no public evidence found that it is a component of another current registry row. Note sample-size caveat remains: GEO describes 240 newly diagnosed IBD cases and 190 controls, but current row records 384 raw / 382 processed samples after exclusions/processing.

Peripheral blood leukocyte/PBMC methylation cohort with IBD cases, controls, and discordant twin sets. Count as independent from DS030/DS031 because public records show a different accession, tissue, and publication context; no subject-level crosswalk was found, so do not claim proven non-overlap beyond public metadata.

Discovery cohort from the pediatric colonic mucosa methylation study. Same publication family as DS031 but public paper/GEO describe discovery and validation cohorts separately; count DS030 once as the discovery cohort, not as duplicate of DS031. One UC subject/sample has before/after treatment structure, so count subjects cautiously.

Validation cohort from the same pediatric colonic mucosa methylation publication as DS030. Count once as a distinct validation cohort, but keep in the same study_family to prevent treating the shared publication/program as unrelated. One UC subject/sample has before/after treatment structure, so count subjects cautiously.

Independent mechanistic methylation resource, but not a direct human IBD patient cohort. Do not count as an independent IBD cohort for cohort-count summaries. Public evidence supports Caco-2 and mouse intestinal epithelial/crypt methylation arrays plus IBD-relevant DNMT3A biology; no overlap risk with patient cohort rows identified.

Controlled-access IIBDGC/NIDDK Global Screening Array GWAS resource. Countability should remain Unclear because public dbGaP states data come from over 30 cohorts / North American IBDGC sites and individual-level plus summary-statistic sharing varies; this is a multi-cohort genetics resource, not a clean single independent cohort. If counted operationally, count once as a GWAS resource only, not as multiple cohorts.

Public sources confirm this is PROTECT pediatric UC rectal methylation. Keep in the PROTECT family with other PROTECT transcriptomic/metabolomic rows. Countability remains Unclear because subject-level overlap with current PROTECT-family rows is likely but not reconciled; do not sum participants/samples across PROTECT rows without sample-ID review.

Paired methylome/transcriptome study of purified/cultured intestinal fibroblasts from Crohn fibrosis and diverticulitis controls. Count once as an independent multi-omic cohort/resource; do not count GEO, SRA, BioProject, RNA-seq, and MiGS/MBD-seq components separately. No public evidence found tying these 6 fibroblast samples to another current cohort row.
